items.label: Development Complete — Backend
messages: Now let me look at the existing patterns to follow:
Good. I can see the barrel export already references `health.js` but I need to check if the file exists:

The schema and OpenAPI spec are already done (likely by infra-dev). Now I need to create: 1. `fn-health.ts` — the Azure Function 2. `fn-health.test.ts` — unit tests 3. Integration test blocks in `smoke.integration.test.ts`  Let me verify the schemas package builds first, then create the function:
Good. Now let me create the function:
Now add integration tests to the existing smoke integration test file:
Now let me run the tests:
All 26 tests pass (10 skipped are the integration tests that only run with `RUN_INTEGRATION=true`). Let me now run type-checking and lint:
Now verify the esbuild output is loadable:
All entry points load successfully. The warnings are expected (we're outside the Azure Functions runtime). Now let me run the roam review and commit:
No breaking changes, no new issues. Let me now commit and complete:
Now leave a doc-note and mark complete:

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/backend && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 && echo "TSC OK" && npm run lint 2>&1 && echo "LINT OK"

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/backend && npm run build 2>&1 && echo "---BUILD OK---" && for f in /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/backend/dist/src/functions/fn-*.js; do echo "Testing: $f
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh backend "feat(health): add fn-health anonymous endpoint with unit and integration tests"

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:doc-note health-badge backend-dev "Added GET /health anonymous Azure Function (fn-health.ts) returning {status:'ok',timestamp} with HealthRe
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:complete health-badge backend-dev
